diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index 4c721f2..0450e46 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -300,2 +300,21 @@
   - Did not start another R&D tier this week; A tier 4 raises immediate compute risk and cash should be preserved until the new segment tests read out.
 - Watch next week: A tier 4 compute, whether A $109 restores S2 conversion, D_S07/D_S04 acquisition economics, churn after the $119 week, and tier 6 headroom.
+
+## Week 19 / Day 126
+
+- Cash: $1,549,014. Subscribers: 131,936. Week 18 added 15,789 subscribers from 37,362 leads; cancellations stayed high at 2,042.
+- Tier 3 R&D completed during the week, lifting global bonus to 0.3284. Broad quality improved, especially with temporary tier 4 plans.
+- Tier 4 compute was too expensive: compute hit -$1,774,618 for the week. Total weekly subscription payments were $3,288,537, but total operating costs exceeded revenue.
+- S2 paid conversion recovered at A $109 and tier 4: S2 content produced 23,109 A leads and 13,990 subscribers, about 60.5%. The issue is not conversion, it is compute and S2 market saturation.
+- D_S07/D_S04 tests failed at current offer/channel mix. D_S04 content/search/network produced 5,627 lost and 0 subscribed. D_S07 produced 4,856 lost and 1 subscribed. Stopped paid spend to those groups.
+- Issues: S2 has 12,230 open, avg age 0.60 days, max 69. Support is still mostly fresh but aging upward.
+- Changes:
+  - Rolled model tiers back to A3/B3/C4 to cut compute roughly in half while keeping S2 quality high after the tier 3 R&D boost.
+  - Kept A/B/C prices at $109/$279/$499.
+  - Stopped all D_S07/D_S04 paid acquisition and removed their lead promos.
+  - Kept S2 content ads at $80,000/day because still profitable.
+  - Reduced total ops to $106,500/day: global $20,000, S2 $85,000, S3 $1,000, S1 $500.
+  - Reduced dev to maintenance/focus: total $4,300/day, mostly S2 with some S3.
+  - Kept capacity tier 6 for headroom.
+  - No new R&D this week; preserve cash while existing tier 4/5/6 projects mature.
+- Watch next week: cash recovery after compute rollback, S2 conversion with A3 post-R&D quality, churn after the $119/tier4 week, issue age, and whether S2 cap starts to sharply limit leads.
